rules.title: Missing Section 164 CrPC/Section 183 BNSS Statement
rules.description: Victim's statement must be recorded before a Magistrate under Section 164 CrPC (now Section 183 BNSS) as mandated for sexual offence cases

remediation.summary: Record victim's statement under Section 164 CrPC/183 BNSS before a Judicial Magistrate
steps: Apply to the Judicial Magistrate for recording the victim's statement
Ensure statement is recorded in the victim's own words without influence
Obtain certified copy of the recorded statement
Reference the statement date, magistrate name, and court in the chargesheet

rules.title: Missing Medical Examination Report
rules.description: Medical examination of the victim must be conducted and the report referenced in the chargesheet as per Section 164A CrPC/Section 184 BNSS

remediation.summary: Ensure medical examination is conducted and report is included
steps: Get victim examined by a registered medical practitioner within 24 hours
Ensure examination is conducted with victim's consent (or guardian's consent for minors)
Obtain the medical examination report with doctor's findings
Include reference to the medical report in evidence schedule with date and doctor name

rules.title: Missing Women Police Officer Involvement
rules.description: Investigation of sexual offences must involve a woman police officer as per statutory requirements

remediation.summary: Ensure involvement of a woman police officer in the investigation
steps: Assign a woman police officer to assist in the investigation
Ensure victim's statement is recorded by or in the presence of a woman officer
Document the woman officer's name and role in the chargesheet
Victim's medical examination must be attended by a woman officer

rules.title: Missing Victim Identity Protection Compliance
rules.description: Chargesheet must comply with Section 228A IPC/Section 72 BNS provisions protecting the identity of the victim of sexual offences

remediation.summary: Ensure victim identity protection measures are in place
steps: Do not disclose victim's name or identity in the chargesheet beyond what is necessary for court proceedings
Refer to the victim as 'prosecutrix' or by initials where possible
Ensure no details that could lead to identification are included in publicly accessible portions
Add a note referencing compliance with Section 228A IPC/Section 72 BNS

rules.title: Missing DNA/Forensic Evidence Reference
rules.description: Sexual offence cases should reference DNA profiling or forensic analysis of biological evidence

remediation.summary: Include forensic evidence references in the chargesheet
steps: Collect biological samples and send to FSL for DNA profiling
Reference FSL report number and date in evidence schedule
Document chain of custody for all biological evidence
If FSL report is pending, note the date samples were sent and expected timeline

rules.title: Missing Age Verification for POCSO Cases
rules.description: If victim is a minor, age verification documents must be referenced and POCSO Act sections must be invoked

remediation.summary: Verify victim's age and apply POCSO provisions if applicable
steps: Obtain birth certificate or school records for age verification
If documents unavailable, request ossification test
Invoke POCSO Act sections alongside BNS/IPC sections if victim is under 18
Reference age proof documents in the chargesheet evidence schedule
